title: Unsafe list access assumptions on transfer operation results
description: The code makes unsafe assumptions about the length of lists returned from transfer operations, using head/tail or index access without checking that the list has the expected number of elements. If a transfer returns fewer results than expected (e.g., due to edge cases), this would cause a runtime crash.
recommendation: Replace unsafe list access with safe pattern matching or explicit length checks before indexing.

oracle.description: List access (head, !!, tail) is used on transfer results without length validation, risking runtime errors on empty or short lists.
oracle.static_signal: Use of 'head', '!!', or 'tail' on a list without prior length check or pattern match; especially on return values from external operations
oracle.symbolic_property: forall r in transferResults: length(r) >= expected_count before access

notes: Common DAML/Haskell bug: using partial functions (head, !!) on potentially empty lists. Easily detectable by static analysis.
